description: State election integrity scorecard data. Scores are illustrative placeholders until primary-source-verified.

stf.name: Staffing
stf.description: Adequate, trained personnel at state and local levels. Funding stability, training mandates, protection from threats/harassment.

mch.name: Voting Machines
mch.description: Security posture of voting equipment: certification, paper-based systems, prohibition of internet-connected tabulators.

dsc.name: Official Discretion
dsc.description: Constraints on local officials' ability to decertify, delay, or selectively count results. Clear mandatory certification statutes.

aud.name: Audit Procedures
aud.description: Post-election audit rigor. Risk-limiting audits (RLAs), chain-of-custody, transparency, public observability.

ppr.name: Paper Trail
ppr.description: Durable, voter-verifiable paper records for every ballot cast. Retention periods and physical security of paper.

rol.name: Voter Rolls
rol.description: Accuracy and integrity of registration lists. Process transparency, due-process protections for removal, ERIC participation.

box.name: Ballot Logistics
box.description: Security of drop boxes, mail-ballot return, and chain of custody from voter to tabulation.

confidence_levels.secondary: Based on secondary sources (news, advocacy orgs)
confidence_levels.primary: Verified against primary sources (statutes, state election websites)

stf.note: Limited state funding; no mandatory training statute

aud.note: Post-election audit required but not RLA

rol.note: Aggressive list maintenance; not ERIC member

dsc.note: Cochise County 2022 certification refusal; legislation pending

mch.note: Paper-based; state certification on top of EAC

box.note: Universal mail ballots; tracked drop boxes
Mail-ballot state with robust drop box security

stf.note: SB 202 penalties chill admin; takeover provisions

dsc.note: State board takeover of local boards

aud.note: RLA pilots; concerns re: BMD auditability

ppr.note: QR-code printouts (not text voters can verify)

box.note: Drop boxes restricted to early-voting sites

mch.note: DRE systems still used in many counties
DRE-only, no paper trail (only state lacking)

aud.note: No meaningful audit possible without paper

box.note: Prop 2 expanded drop boxes with security
Very restrictive absentee; no drop boxes

mch.note: Legacy DRE in some counties; transitioning

dsc.note: Strong certification; Otero County issues resolved

box.note: One drop box per county rule
Very restrictive absentee; no drop boxes

mch.note: DRE in many counties (no paper trail)

ppr.note: No paper trail in many counties

stf.note: County-run; SB1 added penalties chilling admin

dsc.note: SB1 added grounds for challenge; state takeover of Harris Co.

box.note: One drop box per county; SB1 restrictions
Drop boxes restricted by court 2022, then restored 2024
